pg109-p1-s1: His drinking may be constant or it may be heavy only on certain occasions.
pg109-p1-s2: Perhaps he spends too much money for liquor.
pg109-p1-s3: It may be slowing him up mentally and physically, but he does not see it.
pg109-p1-s4:
Sometimes he is a source of embarrassment to
you and his friends.
pg109-p1-s5:
He is positive he can handle his liquor, that it does him no harm, that drinking is necessary in
his business.
pg109-p1-s6: He would probably be insulted if he were called an alcoholic.
pg109-p1-s7: This world is full of people like him.
pg109-p1-s8:
Some
will moderate or stop altogether, and some will not.
pg109-p1-s9:
Of those who keep on, a good number will become true alcoholics
after a while.
pg109-p1:
His drinking may be constant or it may be heavy only on certain occasions. Perhaps he spends too much money for liquor. It may be slowing him up mentally and physically, but he does not see it. Sometimes he is a source of embarrassment to
you and his friends. He is positive he can handle his liquor, that it does him no harm, that drinking is necessary in
his business. He would probably be insulted if he were called an alcoholic. This world is full of people like him. Some
will moderate or stop altogether, and some will not. Of those who keep on, a good number will become true alcoholics
after a while.
pg109-p2-s1: Two: Your husband is showing lack of control, for he is unable to stay on the water wagon even when he wants to.
pg109-p2-s2:
He
often gets entirely out of hand when drinking.
pg109-p2-s3: He admits this is true, but is positive that he will do better.
pg109-p2-s4:
He has
begun to try, with or without your cooperation, various means of moderating or staying dry.
pg109-p2-s5:
Maybe he is beginning to
lose his friends.
pg109-p2-s7:
He is worried at times, and is becoming aware that he cannot drink
like other people.
pg109-p2-s8: He sometimes drinks in the morning and through the day also, to hold his nervousness in check.
pg109-p2-s9:
He is
remorseful after serious drinking bouts and tells you he wants to stop.
pg109-p2-s10:
But when he gets over the spree, he begins to
think once more how he can drink moderately next time.
pg109-p2-s11: We think this person is in danger.
pg109-p2-s12:
These are the earmarks of a
real alcoholic.
pg109-p2-s13: Perhaps he can still tend to business fairly well.
pg109-p2-s14: He has by no means ruined everything.
pg109-p2-s15:
As we say among
ourselves, "He wants to want to stop."
pg109-p2:
Two: Your husband is showing lack of control, for he is unable to stay on the water wagon even when he wants to. He
often gets entirely out of hand when drinking. He admits this is true, but is positive that he will do better. He has
begun to try, with or without your cooperation, various means of moderating or staying dry. Maybe he is beginning to
lose his friends. His business may suffer somewhat. He is worried at times, and is becoming aware that he cannot drink
like other people. He sometimes drinks in the morning and through the day also, to hold his nervousness in check. He is
remorseful after serious drinking bouts and tells you he wants to stop. But when he gets over the spree, he begins to
think once more how he can drink moderately next time. We think this person is in danger. These are the earmarks of a
real alcoholic. Perhaps he can still tend to business fairly well. He has by no means ruined everything. As we say among
ourselves, "He wants to want to stop."
pg109-p3-s1: Three: This husband has gone much further than husband number two.
pg109-p3: Three: This husband has gone much further than husband number two. Though once like number two
